MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Feb-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - PLAINTIFF PAUL CAREY's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD.

Jon-Paul Moorehead and Law Offices of Richard W. Meier's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Plaintiff Paul Carey Jr. is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, the motion is granted.

Plaintiff's protest regarding inadequate service is without merit. On 12/17/2025, Estefani Marquez swore under penalty of perjury in a proper proof of service she served the motion, declaration and proposed order on Plaintiff by overnight mail. Plaintiff's 12/26/2025 Declaration he was never served is not, itself, accompanied by any proof of service. The court credit's Marquez's testimony. In any event, clear is the fact Plaintiff had actual notice of the motion no later than 12/21/2025, the date he prepared his "Formal. Opposition." If Plaintiff had not yet been served, he had plenty of time to follow up. Any failure to do so is on him.

No later than 8:30 am on 2/4/2025, Moving Party must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org an updated proposed order on MC-053 with accurate information inserted, where appropriate. Note the Order will not be effective until Counsel files a Proof of Service of the executed order.

The Court signs two orders.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2025 Continued To Mar-03-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition
Off Calendar Oct-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Paramount Hotels, Inc.'s unopposed Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition is GRANTED.

Moving Defendant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.450. Subdivision (a) of that section provides: "If, after service of a deposition notice, a party to the action or an officer, director, managing agent, or employee of a party, or a person designated by an organization that is a party under Section 2025.230, without having served a valid objection under Section 2025.410, fails to appear for examination, or to proceed with it, or to produce for inspection any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice, the party giving the notice may move for an order compelling the deponent's attendance and testimony, and the production for inspection of any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice."

Moving Defendant served Plaintiff Paul Carey with a deposition notice and Plaintiff (repeatedly) failed to appear or otherwise proceed with the deposition. Plaintiff's failure to appear or proceed is not excused.

Plaintiff is ordered to appear for deposition consistent with the terms set out in the notice on a date time agreed to by Plaintiff and Moving Defendant before November 4, 2025; absent agreement Plaintiff must appear for deposition on November 4, 2025, at 10:00 am at the address identified in the notice.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
